channel.title: Wetown Electric Group - Global Power Distribution & Energy Storage Solutions on ElecPower - Wetown Industrial Power Solutions
channel.description: Recent content in Wetown Electric Group - Global Power Distribution & Energy Storage Solutions on ElecPower - Wetown Industrial Power Solutions

item.title: IPB vs Busbar Trunking (IEC 61439‑6): Key Differences
item.description:
IPB vs Busbar Trunking: Key Differences

Isolated Phase Bus (IPB) and Busbar Trunking (BTS) serve different functions in power distribution systems based on conductor arrangement, enclosure design, and application domain.

item.title: Where Are Busbar Systems Commonly Used?
item.description:
Where Are Busbar Systems Commonly Used?

Busbar systems provide efficient power distribution solutions across various facility types based on current rating requirements and distribution architecture.

item.title: Technical Overview of Isolated Phase Bus (IPB)
item.description:
Technical Overview of Wetown Isolated Phase Bus (IPB)

Wetown Isolated Phase Bus (IPB) is a type of electrical busbar system used for high-current power distribution in industrial facilities, power plants, and large commercial buildings. It provides a reliable and efficient method for transmitting large amounts of electrical power.

item.title: Elements
item.description:
Table of Contents

    1. Heading 3

    12. Blockquote
    13. Tables
    14. Image
    15. Gallery
    16. Slider
    17. Youtube video
    18. Custom video

Here is an example of headings. You can use this heading by the following markdown rules. For example: use # for heading 1 and use ###### for heading 6.

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.description:
Introduction

Wetown Electric Group Co., Ltd. (“we,” “us,” or “our”) respects your privacy and is committed to protecting your personal data. This privacy policy will inform you about how we look after your personal data when you visit our website and tell you about your privacy rights and how the law protects you.
